reasoning_chain_text: 1. In the 2016 city health release, identify Texas cities with diabetes rates above 12%.
2. In the 2017 city health release, keep Texas cities with diabetes rates above 12% again.
3. In the 2018 city health release, keep Texas cities with diabetes rates above 12% again.
4. In the 2019 city health release, intersect the four annual diabetes sets to keep cities meeting the threshold every year.
5. From those cities, keep the ones with 2020 population under 100,000.
6. Determine which city borders both cities remaining after step 5 and record the county containing that city.
7. Retrieve the 2022 percentage aged 65 or older for that county.
original_final_question: What percentage of the population in the county containing the city that borders both of the Texas cities with above 12% diabetes rates between 2016 and 2019 was aged 65 or older in 2022? (These cities had populations under 100,000 in 2020.). Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=4, CDC 500 Cities diabetes data 2016-2019 - nodes 1-4):
Node 1: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2016 release -> 10 cities
Node 2: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2017 release -> 12 cities
Node 3: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2018 release -> 12 cities (Edinburg dropped, Longview/Tyler added)
Node 4: TX cities with diabetes > 12% in 2019 release -> 13 cities (Edinburg returned, Waco added)
Intersection of nodes 1-4: Cities meeting criterion all 4 years -> 9 cities (Beaumont, Brownsville, Corpus Christi, El Paso, Laredo, McAllen, Mission, Pharr, San Antonio)
Node 5: Filter 9-city intersection by 2020 Census pop < 100k -> Mission (85,778) and Pharr (79,715)
Node 6 (Wikipedia Pharr,_Texas): Cities bordering Pharr -> McAllen, Edinburg, San Juan, Hidalgo
Node 7 (Wikipedia Mission,_Texas): Cities bordering Mission -> McAllen, Palmhurst, Palmview
Intersection: City that borders BOTH Pharr and Mission -> McAllen
Node 8: McAllen is in Hidalgo County, Texas (most populous city in the county)
HOP 5 (d=1, data.gov Texas Adult Populations at Risk - node 9):
Node 9: Hidalgo County had 12.3% of population aged 65 and older in 2022
Final answer: 12.3 (percentage of Hidalgo County population aged 65+ in 2022)
